---
title: "Benchmarking Results"
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path

# Try to load benchmark results
results_dir = Path("../benchmarking/results")
csv_files = list(results_dir.glob("benchmark_*.csv"))

if csv_files:
    # Load the most recent results file
    latest_file = max(csv_files, key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)
    df = pd.read_csv(latest_file)
    df = df[df["completed"] == True].copy()
    has_data = len(df) > 0
else:
    has_data = False
    df = None
```

This page presents results from spaceprime benchmark runs. If no benchmark results are available, example interpretations are shown.

## Data Overview

```{python}
#| label: data-overview

if has_data:
    print(f"Loaded {len(df)} benchmark results from {latest_file.name}")
    print(f"\nParameter ranges:")
    print(f"  Grid sizes: {sorted(df['num_demes'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Max local sizes: {sorted(df['max_local_size'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Migration rates: {sorted(df['migration_rate'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Time slices: {sorted(df['time_slices'].unique())}")
else:
    print("No benchmark results found. Run the benchmarks first:")
    print("  cd benchmarking && python run_benchmarks.py --config quick")
```

## Scaling Analysis

### Time vs. Number of Demes

The relationship between grid size and computation time shows how spaceprime scales with landscape complexity.

```{python}
#| label: scaling-plot
#| fig-cap: "Demography setup time scales roughly quadratically with the number of demes."

if has_data:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

    # Group by num_demes
    grouped = df.groupby("num_demes").agg({
        "demography_setup_time_sec": ["mean", "std"],
        "total_time_sec": ["mean", "std"],
    })

    x = grouped.index.values

    # Left plot: Demography setup time
    ax = axes[0]
    y = grouped["demography_setup_time_sec"]["mean"].values
    yerr = grouped["demography_setup_time_sec"]["std"].values
    ax.errorbar(x, y, yerr=yerr, fmt="o-", capsize=3, label="Setup time")
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel("Number of Demes")
    ax.set_ylabel("Time (seconds)")
    ax.set_title("Demography Setup Time")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Right plot: Total time
    ax = axes[1]
    y = grouped["total_time_sec"]["mean"].values
    yerr = grouped["total_time_sec"]["std"].values
    ax.errorbar(x, y, yerr=yerr, fmt="s-", capsize=3, color="C1")
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel("Number of Demes")
    ax.set_ylabel("Time (seconds)")
    ax.set_title("Total Benchmark Time")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No data available for scaling plot.")
```

### Grid Size × Time Slices Heatmap

This heatmap shows how computation time varies across combinations of spatial and temporal complexity.

```{python}
#| label: heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Total time increases with both grid size and number of time slices."

if has_data and len(df["time_slices"].unique()) > 1:
    df_plot = df.copy()
    df_plot["grid_size"] = df_plot["grid_rows"].astype(str) + "x" + df_plot["grid_cols"].astype(str)

    pivot = df_plot.pivot_table(
        values="total_time_sec",
        index="grid_size",
        columns="time_slices",
        aggfunc="mean",
    )

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
    im = ax.imshow(pivot.values, aspect="auto", cmap="YlOrRd")
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(pivot.columns)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(pivot.columns)
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(pivot.index)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(pivot.index)
    ax.set_xlabel("Time Slices")
    ax.set_ylabel("Grid Size")
    ax.set_title("Mean Total Time (seconds)")

    cbar = plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax)
    cbar.set_label("Time (s)")

    # Add annotations
    for i in range(len(pivot.index)):
        for j in range(len(pivot.columns)):
            value = pivot.values[i, j]
            if not np.isnan(value):
                text_color = "white" if value > pivot.values.max() / 2 else "black"
                ax.text(j, i, f"{value:.2f}", ha="center", va="center",
                       color=text_color, fontsize=8)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("Insufficient data for heatmap (need multiple time slices).")
```

## Parameter Effects

### Individual Parameter Impact

Understanding how each parameter affects performance helps optimize simulation design.

```{python}
#| label: parameter-effects
#| fig-cap: "Effect of individual parameters on total computation time."

if has_data:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))

    # Effect of max_local_size
    ax = axes[0, 0]
    if len(df["max_local_size"].unique()) > 1:
        grouped = df.groupby("max_local_size")["total_time_sec"].agg(["mean", "std"])
        ax.errorbar(grouped.index, grouped["mean"], yerr=grouped["std"],
                   fmt="o-", capsize=3)
    ax.set_xlabel("Max Local Size")
    ax.set_ylabel("Total Time (s)")
    ax.set_title("Effect of Deme Size")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Effect of migration rate
    ax = axes[0, 1]
    if len(df["migration_rate"].unique()) > 1:
        grouped = df.groupby("migration_rate")["total_time_sec"].agg(["mean", "std"])
        ax.errorbar(grouped.index, grouped["mean"], yerr=grouped["std"],
                   fmt="s-", capsize=3, color="C1")
        ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel("Migration Rate")
    ax.set_ylabel("Total Time (s)")
    ax.set_title("Effect of Migration Rate")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Effect of time slices
    ax = axes[1, 0]
    if len(df["time_slices"].unique()) > 1:
        grouped = df.groupby("time_slices")["total_time_sec"].agg(["mean", "std"])
        ax.errorbar(grouped.index, grouped["mean"], yerr=grouped["std"],
                   fmt="^-", capsize=3, color="C2")
    ax.set_xlabel("Time Slices")
    ax.set_ylabel("Total Time (s)")
    ax.set_title("Effect of Time Slices")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Memory scaling
    ax = axes[1, 1]
    grouped = df.groupby("num_demes")["peak_memory_mb"].agg(["mean", "std", "max"])
    ax.errorbar(grouped.index, grouped["mean"], yerr=grouped["std"],
               fmt="d-", capsize=3, color="C3", label="Mean")
    ax.plot(grouped.index, grouped["max"], "x--", color="C4", label="Max")
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_xlabel("Number of Demes")
    ax.set_ylabel("Peak Memory (MB)")
    ax.set_title("Memory Scaling")
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No data available for parameter effects plot.")
```

## Time Breakdown

Understanding which operations consume the most time helps identify optimization targets.

```{python}
#| label: time-breakdown
#| fig-cap: "Breakdown of time spent in each benchmark component."

if has_data:
    df_plot = df.copy()
    df_plot["grid_size"] = df_plot["grid_rows"].astype(str) + "x" + df_plot["grid_cols"].astype(str)

    grouped = df_plot.groupby("grid_size").agg({
        "migration_matrix_time_sec": "mean",
        "demography_setup_time_sec": "mean",
        "simulation_time_sec": "mean",
    })

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

    x = range(len(grouped))
    width = 0.8

    ax.bar(x, grouped["migration_matrix_time_sec"], width,
           label="Migration Matrix")
    ax.bar(x, grouped["demography_setup_time_sec"], width,
           bottom=grouped["migration_matrix_time_sec"],
           label="Demography Setup")
    ax.bar(x, grouped["simulation_time_sec"], width,
           bottom=grouped["migration_matrix_time_sec"] + grouped["demography_setup_time_sec"],
           label="Simulation")

    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(grouped.index, rotation=45, ha="right")
    ax.set_xlabel("Grid Size")
    ax.set_ylabel("Time (seconds)")
    ax.set_title("Time Breakdown by Component")
    ax.legend()

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No data available for time breakdown plot.")
```

## Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: summary-stats

if has_data:
    # Calculate summary by grid size
    summary = df.groupby(["grid_rows", "grid_cols"]).agg({
        "total_time_sec": ["mean", "std", "min", "max"],
        "peak_memory_mb": ["mean", "max"],
        "num_samples": "first",
    }).round(3)

    print("Summary by Grid Size:")
    print(summary.to_string())
else:
    print("No data available for summary statistics.")
```

## Recommendations

Based on typical benchmark results:

1. **Small studies (< 100 demes)**: Run on local machine, expect < 1 minute setup
2. **Medium studies (100-500 demes)**: Local machine suitable, 1-10 minutes setup
3. **Large studies (> 500 demes)**: Consider HPC resources, may need > 10 minutes setup
4. **Time series with many slices**: Scale roughly linearly with slice count
5. **Memory considerations**: Migration matrix is O(n²), plan accordingly for large grids

## Running Your Own Benchmarks

To generate results specific to your hardware:

```bash
# Quick test
cd benchmarking
python run_benchmarks.py --config quick

# Full suite
python run_benchmarks.py --config default

# Generate analysis
python analyze_results.py results/benchmark_*.csv --output figures/
```
